acceptance_targets: [ch13_f03] PASS if revised dialogue in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:303-320 and 451-456 shows contractions in the majority of eligible clauses for Ravi/Anika, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:351-387 avoids consecutive legalistic declaratives in Priya's speech; FAIL otherwise.

[ch13_f04] PASS if snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:83-191 retains at least three refusal beats while limiting standalone one-word 'No.' lines to no more than two and each remaining refusal adds a distinct new stake; FAIL otherwise.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if revised dialogue in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:303-320 and 451-456 shows contractions in the majority of eligible clauses for Ravi/Anika, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:351-387 avoids consecutive legalistic declaratives in Priya's speech; FAIL otherwise.
findings.problem: Register drifts stiff in high-pressure exchanges through repeated uncontracted/legal phrasing, conflicting with the style-bible default of high contraction use for spoken dialogue.
findings.rewrite_direction: Tighten spoken realism at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:303-320, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:351-387, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:451-456 by using contractions as default and reserving uncontracted forms for a single deliberate emphasis beat per speaker.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:83-191 retains at least three refusal beats while limiting standalone one-word 'No.' lines to no more than two and each remaining refusal adds a distinct new stake; FAIL otherwise.
findings.problem: The refusal sequence overuses standalone 'No.' beats, flattening leverage shifts and making Anika's resistance cadence feel mechanically repeated.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rebuild the confrontation turns at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:83-191 so refusals remain firm but each beat changes leverage (agreement breach, bodily limit, relational boundary, exit consequence) instead of repeating identical one-word closures.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Focus on dialogue realism and voice separation: idiolect fidelity, leverage shifts, pressure cadence, anti-transcript behavior, natural colloquial texture (contractions/interruptions/slang when character-true), and pressure-true profanity usage per aesthetic risk policy.
revision_pass.label: dialogue/idiolect/cadence fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into uncomfortable specificity when it changes behavior: pain detail, debt arithmetic, manipulative care, and mixed motives. In edge cases, prefer the truest in-character action over protective narration, then carry the real consequence forward instead of immediately moralizing it away.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If coercion, grief, injury, or economic shame appears, language must stay specific and embodied; vague softening triggers mandatory rewrite.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High - contractions are the spoken default; uncontracted forms are reserved for formal authority, deliberate emphasis, or emotional freezing.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Vary scene count and dialogue density chapter to chapter: rotate match compression, slow inventory aftermath, institutional pressure scenes, and quiet domestic intervals so adjacent chapters do not share the same structural pulse.
prose_style_profile.diction: Concrete contemporary diction with class-aware specificity; avoid abstract therapy language unless a character would actually use it.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third anchored in Anika for most chapters, with brief controlled slips toward Ravi in designated confrontation and aftermath chapters.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Alternate clipped athletic sequences with longer reflective cadences; sentence length should track exertion, shame, and release.
